commit.message:
perf(meals): instant-feel shopping lists + offline list cache (#241)

* perf(meals): optimistic shopping list updates and offline cache for lists

- Apply TanStack Query optimistic cache updates for grocery items and
  shopping lists so toggles and adds feel instant; use networkMode always
  so flaky supermarket connectivity still attempts mutations.
- Treat SW 202 queued responses like offline queue success; invalidate
  grocery and shopping-lists after SYNC_* messages from the service worker.
- Parse offline-wrapped API GET payloads for grocery and shopping-lists.
- Cache GET /api/shopping-lists in the service worker for offline reads.
- Pass householdId and userId from the grocery RSC page for correct
  optimistic grocery rows; remove isPending gating on add buttons.

* perf(meals): instant checkbox feedback on shopping list rows

- Track pending checked state on click so the UI updates in the same frame
  before TanStack Query cache propagation.
- Pass explicit next is_checked to the mutation; clear pending state in
  onSettled (covers success, error, and offline queue).
- Remove transition-colors from the grocery checkbox for snappier visuals.

---------

anymore

commit.message:
refactor: restructure project to Advanced RAG modular architecture (#3)

Implements the full directory layout described in the architectural spec:

Core changes:
- app/core/: BaseSettings config, logging, security, constants
- app/schemas/: Split into common/document/request/response modules
- app/storage/: VectorStorePort abstraction + QdrantStore + PgVectorStore stub
  - vectorstores/base.py: port interface for easy backend swap
  - vectorstores/qdrant_store.py: concrete Qdrant implementation
  - chat_history, checkpoints, postgres, redis, blob placeholders
- app/providers/: Lazy singletons for LLM/embedding/vectorstore/reranker/cache
- app/graphs/crag/: Full LangGraph CRAG graph
  - state.py: CRAGState with clarification/rewrite/expansion/hallucination fields
  - nodes.py: 10 node functions (analyze, clarify, rewrite, retrieve, expand, etc.)
  - routes.py: conditional edge functions separated from node logic
  - graph.py: StateGraph compile with hallucination feedback loop (max 3x)
- app/rag/: Full pipeline components
  - loaders/, preprocess/, chunkers/ (recursive + semantic + parent-child)
  - query/: query_analyzer, query_rewriter, clarification generator
  - retrievers/: vector, BM25, hybrid (RRF), parent-child, small-to-big, orchestrator
  - rerankers/: CrossEncoder + LLM reranker
  - evaluators/: retrieval, grounding, hallucination evaluators
  - guards/: relevance, hallucination, policy guards
  - citations/: citation_builder + source_formatter
  - policies/: rewrite, clarification, expansion, retry policies (isolated from nodes)
  - pipelines/: ingest_pipeline + retrieval_pipeline
- app/prompts/: 7 YAML prompt templates (replaces prompts.py)
- app/services/: chat, ingest, index, document service layer
- app/workers/: background task stubs
- app/api/v1/: health, ingest, chat routers (split from monolithic routes.py)
- app/api/dependencies.py: FastAPI DI assembly

Tests:
- tests/unit/: 25 tests (query_analyzer, policies, retrievers, nodes)
- tests/integration/: 17 tests (API, graph flow, vectorstore swap)
- All 49 tests pass including backward-compat tests/test_api.py + test_vectorstore.py

Supporting files:
- evals/: datasets (JSONL) + offline eval scripts + regression golden set
- scripts/: ingest.py, rebuild_index.py, run_evals.py
- docker/: Dockerfile + entrypoint
- data/raw/, data/processed/ directories
- Makefile: added test-unit, test-integration, evals targets
- pyproject.toml: added langchain-text-splitters dependency

Backward compatibility shims kept for:
- app/config.py → app/core/config
- app/vectorstore/store.py → app/storage/vectorstores/qdrant_store
- app/api/routes.py → aggregates v1 sub-routers
- app/api/schemas.py → re-exports from app/schemas/*

commit.message:
Refactor: Remove unnecessary lint suppressions (#3317) ### Checklist - [ ] If applicable, unit tests - [ ] If applicable, create follow-up issues for `purchases-ios` and hybrids ### Motivation This PR removes a small, reviewable set of unnecessary suppression annotations by refactoring control flow (without changing behavior). The goal is to reduce suppression noise and improve maintainability. ### Description I audited suppression usage across the repo (`@Suppress`, `@SuppressLint`) and checked for lint baseline files. #### Baseline findings - No `lint-baseline.xml` (or similarly named lint baseline file) is currently tracked. - Lint config files found: - `config/lint/lint.xml` - `ui/revenuecatui/lint.xml` - Static-analysis baseline in this repo is primarily Detekt baseline (`purchases/detekt-baseline.xml`, etc.), not Android lint baseline. #### Suppressions removed (limited to 5) 1. `settings.gradle.kts` - Removed `@Suppress("ReturnCount")` from `isSamsungIAPAARPresent` - Refactor: replaced early-return style with null-safe expression combining version lookup and AAR existence check. 2. `settings.gradle.kts` - Removed `@Suppress("ReturnCount")` from `readVersionFromCatalog` - Refactor: replaced loop with `firstNotNullOfOrNull` to return the matched version expression-style. 3. `purchases/src/main/kotlin/com/revenuecat/purchases/deeplinks/DeepLinkParser.kt` - Removed `@Suppress("ReturnCount")` from `parseWebPurchaseRedemption` - Refactor: converted nested branching to expression-style `if` returning nullable result. 4. `purchases/src/main/kotlin/com/revenuecat/purchases/common/verification/IntermediateSignatureHelper.kt` - Removed `@Suppress("ReturnCount")` from `createIntermediateKeyVerifierIfVerified` - Refactor: converted guard/early returns to expression-style branching while preserving exact error/success outcomes. 5. `purchases/src/main/kotlin/com/revenuecat/purchases/paywalls/fonts/DownloadableFontInfo.kt` - Removed `@Suppress("ReturnCount")` from `toDownloadableFontInfo` - Refactor: consolidated validation into a `when`-derived `error` value, then returned `Result.Error` or `Result.Success` once. #### Verification - ✅ `./gradlew detektAll` passes. - ⚠️ Android lint/unit test tasks requiring Android SDK could not run in this environment: - `:purchases:testDefaultsBc8DebugUnitTest` - `:purchases:lintDefaultsBc8Debug` - Failure reason: missing SDK configuration (`ANDROID_HOME` / `sdk.dir`). These changes are intentionally scoped to exactly 5 suppression removals to keep review manageable.
  
--- > [!NOTE] > **Low Risk** > Behavior should be unchanged, but several functions were refactored from early returns to expression-style control flow, which could subtly alter edge-case return paths if mistakes were introduced. > > **Overview** > Removes several `@Suppress("ReturnCount")` annotations by refactoring control flow to expression-style returns. > > This updates intermediate signature verification, deep link parsing, downloadable font validation, and Gradle settings helpers to return via `if`/`when` expressions (and `firstNotNullOfOrNull`) instead of multiple early returns, while preserving the same success/error outcomes. > > Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 5c2d26e1c6b3d45bad599d47b49c9e5939ee03ed. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot). --------- Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: Cesar de la Vega Co-authored-by: Cesar de la Vega <664544+vegaro@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(repo): Upgrade Flutter to `>=3.38.1` and enable new lints (#29)

* feat(ui): Introduce Stream Design System foundations and gallery app (#31)

* feat: Introduce Stream Design System foundations and gallery app

This commit introduces the foundational elements of the new Stream Design System and a comprehensive gallery application (Widgetbook) for showcasing and customizing components.

**Core Package (`stream_core_flutter`):**

*   **Design Tokens**: Added foundational design tokens for colors, spacing, radius, typography, and box shadows. These tokens are organized into primitive and semantic layers.
    *   `StreamColors`: Defines the primitive color palette.
    *   `StreamColorScheme`: Provides semantic color roles (e.g., `accentPrimary`, `textSecondary`).
    *   `StreamTypography`, `StreamTextTheme`: Defines font primitives and semantic text styles.
    *   `StreamSpacing`, `StreamRadius`, `StreamBoxShadow`: Adds tokens for layout and elevation.
*   **`StreamTheme`**: Implemented a new `ThemeExtension` to aggregate all design tokens and component themes, enabling centralized theming.
*   **New Components**: Added `StreamAvatar`, `StreamAvatarStack`, and `StreamOnlineIndicator`.
*   **Theming Infrastructure**: Added `theme_extensions_builder` to auto-generate `copyWith`, `lerp`, and `==`/`hashCode` for theme classes.

**Design System Gallery (`design_system_gallery`):**

*   **Gallery App**: Replaced the basic Widgetbook setup with a new, fully-featured gallery application (`StreamDesignSystemGallery`).
*   **Theme Studio**: Added a "Theme Studio" panel for real-time customization of all `StreamColorScheme` properties.
*   **Enhanced Previews**:
    *   Added a toolbar with controls for device selection (`DeviceFrame`), text scaling, and light/dark mode toggles.
    *   Created a `PreviewWrapper` to apply theme, device frame, and text scale to all use cases.
*   **New Use Cases**:
    *   Added comprehensive use cases for new components (`StreamAvatar`, `StreamAvatarStack`, `StreamOnlineIndicator`).
    *   Added showcases for semantic tokens (`Typography`, `Elevations`).
    *   Improved `StreamButton` use cases with more variants and real-world examples.
*   **Agent Guide**: Added `AGENTS.md`, a guide for AI agents on contributing to the gallery.

* chore: upgrade React Native 0.85, align Jest preset, pin React 19.2.3

Bump react-native to 0.85.0, babel-plugin-syntax-hermes-parser to ^0.35.0,
and add @react-native/jest-preset for the ReactNative Jest project (resolver,
setup, env, asset transformer, react-native subpath mapper). Pin react,
react-dom, and react-test-renderer to 19.2.3 to match react-native-renderer.
Fix subscriptions.native test import to use @data-client/test.

Co-authored-by: Nathaniel Tucker <me@ntucker.me>

* chore: remove changeset for internal RN/Jest CI update

Co-authored-by: Nathaniel Tucker <me@ntucker.me>

* fix: align package-lock.json react versions to 19.2.3 to match package.json

The lockfiles for github-app, nextjs, and todo-app had react and react-dom
at 19.2.5 while their package.json files pinned 19.2.3. This mismatch
would cause npm ci to fail. Regenerated lockfiles to resolve correctly.

Co-authored-by: Nathaniel Tucker <me@ntucker.me>

* fix: remove redundant slash in react-native moduleNameMapper replacement

The capture group ($|/.*) already includes the leading slash for sub-path
imports like react-native/Libraries/Text, so the extra / in the
replacement template produced double slashes (e.g. <rnDir>//Libraries/Text).

Removing the redundant / fixes both bare react-native (empty capture)
and sub-path imports.

Co-authored-by: Nathaniel Tucker <me@ntucker.me>

feat(prompts): harden SDD planning, clarification, and review workflows (#50)

* feat(prompts): add mandatory planning audit workflow

Enforce planning audit gates between task generation and implementation
so required planning defects are blocked before execution begins.

Add audit-aware auto-selection and planning-resume behavior so existing
specs with tasks can continue through audit/remediation without unwanted
task regeneration.

* docs: document planning audit gate and remediation flow

Clarify that SDD-2 now includes a mandatory planning audit checkpoint
with human-approved remediation before implementation handoff.

Update README workflow and artifact outputs, and extend FAQ with
planning-quality and remediation behavior details.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <no-reply@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* feat(prompts): add compact audit mode and reset gate

Improve SDD-2 audit readability by defaulting to compact Gateboard and
exception-only reporting, with delta-focused re-audit output for faster
human review cycles.

Add a deterministic reset recommendation gate that triggers restart vs
remediation decisions when planning debt exceeds defined thresholds.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <no-reply@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* feat(prompts): harden SDD-2 planning audit reliability

Add explicit standards-discovery and evidence checkpoints so repository guidance files are consistently read before audit decisions.

Introduce a required chain-of-verification step before implementation handoff and define failure handling for missing standards artifacts.

Keep audit output compact with a required standards evidence table and minimal pass-case reporting.

* docs(prompts): refine validation file-integrity gate

Introduce tiered Gate D rules to distinguish blocker-level core file drift from non-blocking supporting-file linkage gaps.

Clarify file integrity expectations across gates, detailed checks, red flags, and report examples to improve validation consistency and traceability.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <no-reply@example.com>

Clarify spec-generation review flow by nesting the cross-domain guard

under Step 6 and replacing undefined terminology with context-aware wording.

sufficiency checks, and add a direct GATE D linkage clarification reference.

Add recommended-answer guidance to generated questions files so users get direction and tradeoff notes when clarification is needed.

fix(plugin-nested-docs): await populateBreadcrumbs in resaveChildren (#15582)

Fixes https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/issues/14943

## Summary

The async `populateBreadcrumbs()` function was not being awaited in
`resaveChildren`. This caused `payload.update()` to receive a Promise
instead of the child document's data, so updates couldn't preserve the
`_status` field and defaulted to the main document's draft status.

When a parent was saved, all child updates created draft versions
instead of preserving the published/draft distinction. Querying with
`draft: false` then returned draft data, making the published version
"disappear."

The fix adds `await` to the `populateBreadcrumbs()` call.

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Jarrod Flesch <jarrodmflesch@gmail.com>

feat(rules): refine adaptive reasoning labels, triggers, and testing guidance

- Rename light/deep tiers to "Raciocínio Leve/Profundo" and align pipeline wording
- Tighten "Profundo" criteria (2+ files, Source of Truth, architecture, design, etc.)
- Add testing-policy reminder and validation/test checks in interaction and guardrails

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat(cssHelper): support css`` ternary branches with inner theme indexed lookup (#374)

* feat(cssHelper): support css`` ternary branches with inner theme indexed lookup

Handle the pattern where a conditional `css` tagged template has inner
ternaries with computed theme access (e.g. `props.theme.color[props.color]`).
Falls back to template literal resolution when css-helper can't resolve
to static values, and extends `resolveDynamicTemplateExpr` to resolve
indexed theme lookups in ConditionalExpression branches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cssHelper): narrow background→backgroundColor to ConditionalExpression only

The previous check used `condition === "always"` which also matches
destructured defaults (e.g. `({ bg = "url(...)" }) => background: ${bg}`).
Adding a `callArg.type === "ConditionalExpression"` guard ensures only
theme-resolved conditionals (where both branches are color tokens) get
the backgroundColor conversion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cssHelper): remove unsafe background→backgroundColor shorthand conversion

Remove the special case that converted `background: ${expr}` to
`backgroundColor` for theme-resolved conditionals. The conversion was
unsafe for non-color theme tokens (gradients, images) since
`extractIndexedThemeLookupInfo` matches any theme path, not just
`theme.color`. The `background` shorthand now correctly falls through
to the inline style path which preserves full CSS shorthand semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "fix(cssHelper): remove unsafe background→backgroundColor shorthand conversion"

This reverts commit 4830a2656cf4cd8c0c39186dbbb4900d697eb99d.

* fix(background): remove unsafe background→backgroundColor forcing for dynamic theme values (#381)

Dynamic `background: ${expr}` interpolations with indexed theme lookups
were unconditionally mapped to backgroundColor, which drops rendering
semantics when theme values are gradients/images instead of colors.
Now falls through to the inline style path which preserves the CSS
background shorthand and works correctly for both colors and images.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add deploy-adapter command for user-controlled LoRA deployment (#191)

* feat: storage-first flow - 0G Storage first with TEE fallback

- Dataset upload: try 0G Storage first, fallback to TEE on failure
- Model download: default 'auto' mode tries 0G Storage first, TEE fallback
- Upload function now returns root hash from 0g-storage-client output
- Add 'auto' download method (default) alongside 'tee' and '0g-storage'
- Update 0g-storage-client binary to latest main (fixes contract ABI)

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: add missing verifyDownloadedModelHash method for TEE download hash verification

The method was missing after cherry-pick, causing TypeScript build failure.
This method verifies downloaded model hash against on-chain modelRootHash
when using TEE fallback download.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: resolve 0G Storage download failure when data-path is a directory

When data-path points to an existing directory, 0G Storage client fails
with "is a directory" error. Now auto-appends a filename (model_{taskId}.bin)
when the path is a directory, so 0G Storage client can create the output file.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: address code review feedback from Claude

- [HIGH] Reject if root hash is empty after successful upload in zg-storage
- [MEDIUM] Replace console.log/error with logger in SDK code (dataset.ts, zg-storage.ts)
- [LOW] Fix JSDoc: default downloadMethod is 'auto', not '0g-storage'
- [LOW] Improve CLI --download-method option description formatting

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* feat: add CLI commands for LoRA inference testing

- Add makeAdapterName utility for consistent LoRA adapter naming
- Add `fine-tuning get-adapter-name` command
- Add `fine-tuning chat` command with --adapter-name option
- Add `inference get-secret --duration` for non-interactive API key generation

These commands enable CLI-driven E2E testing of the LoRA serving flow.

Made-with: Cursor

* feat(cli): add deploy-adapter command and --deploy flag for acknowledge-model

New command `fine-tuning deploy-adapter` triggers adapter deployment
to the inference GPU via the broker's new /v1/lora/adapters/deploy API.

The `acknowledge-model` command now accepts `--deploy` to automatically
wait for the broker to download the adapter and deploy it in one step.

Without --deploy, acknowledge only downloads the model; users must
call deploy-adapter separately when they want to go live.

* fix(cli): adapter name resolution and .option() syntax bugs

- Fix deploy-adapter to resolve adapter name from broker API by taskId
  (handles base model name mismatch between CLI and broker internal paths)
- Add --broker-url option to deploy-adapter for direct broker targeting
  (bypasses contract lookup in dev/2-CVM setups)
- Fix malformed .option() syntax in model-usage and download commands
  (template literal used incorrectly as first argument to Commander)

Tested: full E2E flow with real fine-tuning, deploy, and inference
on 2-CVM testnet setup.

* fix(cli): add --broker-url support to acknowledge-model --deploy

In 2-CVM setups where the fine-tuning provider differs from the
inference provider, the --deploy flag on acknowledge-model failed
because it tried to look up the inference broker URL using the
fine-tuning provider address. Adding --broker-url allows users to
specify the inference broker URL directly.

* fix(cli): resolve adapter name from broker for chat and deploy commands

- chat command now queries broker by taskId to get the real adapter name,
  fixing 400 errors when broker uses path-based model names
- deploy-adapter polling loop re-resolves adapter name dynamically,
  fixing timeout when called right after acknowledge (before broker
  processes the on-chain event)

* fix: use withBroker for deploy-adapter and chat commands

These commands need getServiceMetadata/getRequestHeaders which are
not available on ReadOnlyInferenceBroker.

* refactor(sdk): move LoRA adapter business logic from CLI to SDK

- New `LoRAProcessor` class in `src.ts/sdk/inference/broker/lora.ts`
  with typed interfaces (AdapterInfo, AdapterStatusResponse, etc.)
- New SDK methods on InferenceBroker: deployAdapter(), getAdapterStatus(),
  listAdapters(), resolveAdapterName(), chatWithFineTunedModel()
- CLI deploy-adapter and chat commands are now thin wrappers calling SDK
- Eliminated 3x duplicated adapter name resolution logic
- Replaced all `any` types with proper TypeScript interfaces
- Extracted magic numbers to named constants

Addresses code review feedback from Claude bot on PR #191.

* refactor(sdk): use interface for LoRA deps, remove --broker-url

- Replace function params with `LoRADependencies` interface for cleaner DI
- Fix `as unknown as Record<string, string>` type cast with proper
  Object.entries conversion
- Remove --broker-url CLI option from deploy-adapter and acknowledge-model
  (broker URL is always resolved from on-chain service metadata)

Addresses HONGJICAI's review comments on PR #191.

* fix(sdk): use single shared deadline in deployAdapter()

The two polling phases (wait for ready + wait for active) now share
one deadline computed at the top, so actual max wait matches the
user-specified timeout instead of potentially doubling it.

Addresses HONGJICAI's review comment on PR #191.

* fix: trim leading dashes in adapter name + add --adapter-name to deploy-adapter

1. Fixed double-dash bug in makeAdapterName: when baseModel starts with
   "/" (e.g. "/models/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct"), sanitization produced
   a leading dash causing "ft--models-..." instead of "ft-models-...".
   Added .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '') to trim.

2. Added --adapter-name option to deploy-adapter CLI command as the
   primary way to identify adapters, with --model + --task-id as fallback.
   This is consistent with the chat command which already supports it.

3. Added deployAdapterByName() SDK method that sends {adapterName}
   directly to the broker deploy API.

Addresses review feedback from HONGJICAI.

* chore: remove accidentally committed files

Remove binary/0g-storage-client.bak and ack-tee-signer.cjs that were
swept up by git add -A in the previous commit.

* fix: address code review feedback from Ravenyjh

1. [BUG] Add missing 'failed' state check after first polling loop
   in deployAdapter. Previously, a failed adapter during download
   would fall through and send an unnecessary deploy POST.

2. [CLAUDE.md] Replace untyped Function with explicit signatures
   in deployAdapterToBroker and deployAdapterByName helpers.

* fix: add session auth headers to LoRA management API calls

Send Authorization header on deploy, status, list, and resolve calls
so the broker can authenticate the user. Companion change to
0g-serving-broker PR #379 which re-enables ValidateSession on these
endpoints.

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `erpc dump` command (#765)

* feat: add `erpc dump` command and make ScoreMultiplier fields optional

- Add `erpc dump <config-file>` command to parse TS/JS/YAML config and
  output the resolved configuration as YAML or JSON
- Support `--with-defaults` flag to apply eRPC defaults before dumping
- Support `--format yaml|json` flag for output format
- Add LoadConfigRaw() for parsing config without defaults/validation
- Add MarshalYAML() for Duration, DataFinalityState, CacheEmptyBehavior,
  CachePolicyAppliesTo, AvailbilityConfidence, RateLimitPeriod, and
  SelectionPolicyConfig
- Fix RateLimitPeriod.UnmarshalYAML to try integer enum before string
  (fixes parsing of `period: 1` in generated YAML)
- Make ScoreMultiplierConfig.Network and Method optional (omitempty),
  defaulting to wildcard "*" via SetDefaults()
- Regenerate TypeScript types with optional network/method fields

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: SelectionPolicyConfig.MarshalYAML handles TS/JS eval functions

When config is loaded from TypeScript/JS, EvalFunction is a compiled
callable but evalFunctionOriginal is empty. MarshalYAML now checks
EvalFunction != nil and outputs "<function>" as a placeholder, matching
the existing MarshalJSON behavior. Also omits zero-value intervals.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* simplify: drop --with-defaults flag from dump command

Raw dump is the primary use case (comparing TS output against prod YAML).
Defaults add noise and fail when env vars are missing.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: reject unsupported --format values in dump command

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: bounds-check enum String() methods, add omitempty to JSON tags

- DataFinalityState.String() and CacheEmptyBehavior.String() now return
  "invalid(N)" instead of panicking on out-of-range values
- ScoreMultiplierConfig.Network/Method JSON tags now include omitempty
  to match YAML tag behavior

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* feat: add --defaults flag to dump command

When --defaults is passed, applies SetDefaults() to the parsed config
before dumping. This shows the final resolved config with all eRPC
defaults applied, which is what users want to see when validating
their config against production behavior.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: rename --defaults to --with-defaults in dump command

The flag was incorrectly named 'defaults' but the PR description documents it as '--with-defaults', causing a mismatch between the documented CLI interface and actual implementation.

* simplify: always apply defaults in dump, remove --with-defaults flag

There's no use case for dumping raw config without defaults. The dump
command now always applies SetDefaults before output.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* docs: add CLI commands section with validate and dump usage

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: add --with-defaults flag to dump command for optional defaults application

* Revert "fix: add --with-defaults flag to dump command for optional defaults application"

This reverts commit cd03bbc2f9736e3036975eb7005aa69ae86458e0.

* docs: update LoadConfigRaw comment to reflect dump always applies defaults

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* refactor: remove LoadConfigRaw, use LoadConfig everywhere

There's no valid use case for loading config without defaults and
validation. The dump command now goes through the same pipeline as
production: load + SetDefaults + Validate. This ensures dump output
reflects exactly what eRPC would accept at startup.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* chore: simplify LoadConfig comment

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: redact secrets in YAML dump output, fix SelectionPolicyConfig MarshalJSON, reuse getConfig in dump

- Add MarshalYAML methods for RedisConnectorConfig, PostgreSQLConnectorConfig,
  AwsAuthConfig, ProviderConfig, UpstreamConfig, and SecretStrategyConfig to
  redact sensitive fields (passwords, URIs, API keys) in YAML output, matching
  existing MarshalJSON redaction behavior
- Fix SelectionPolicyConfig.MarshalJSON: use else-if so evalFunctionOriginal
  source is preserved instead of being overwritten by "<function>"
- Refactor dump command to reuse getConfig() instead of duplicating config
  loading logic, enabling --config flag and default config path resolution
- Regenerate TypeScript types after rebase onto main

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: include rateLimitBudget in SecretStrategyConfig.MarshalYAML

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Usage page shows stale cached data until manual refresh (#4057) ## Summary Fixes the Usage page showing stale cached data when users navigate to it, requiring a manual browser refresh to see updated information. ## Root Cause The `useOrganizationUsage` hook had `staleTime: 30 * 1000` (30 seconds), which meant React Query considered data "fresh" for 30 seconds after fetching. Since the `OrganizationMenuButton` component also subscribes to the same query key and is always mounted in the navigation, the query cache stayed warm. When users navigated to the Usage page, React Query served the cached data without refetching. ## Fix - Set `staleTime: 0` so data is always considered stale and refetched when any subscriber mounts (i.e., when navigating to the Usage page). - Added `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` to avoid unnecessary refetches on tab switches while still ensuring fresh data on navigation. This follows the exact same pattern already used by `useCanvas` and `useCanvasExecutions` in `useCanvasData.ts`. ## Changes - `web_src/src/hooks/useOrganizationData.ts`: Updated `useOrganizationUsage` query options. ## Impact - **Usage page**: Always shows fresh data when navigated to. - **OrganizationMenuButton**: Also benefits — the usage status shown in the nav menu will be refreshed whenever the menu component remounts. - **Other pages**: No impact — this change is scoped to the usage query only. Closes #4056
  
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent

feat: Windows Server 2019 内网离线部署方案 (#2)

* feat: Windows Server 2019 内网离线部署方案

部署架构:
- 后端 Express 在生产模式下直接托管前端静态文件，单端口运行
- 在有网络的机器上使用 prepare 脚本构建部署包
- 部署包包含全部运行时依赖，目标服务器只需安装 Node.js

新增文件:
- deploy/prepare.bat  - Windows 构建脚本（需网络）
- deploy/prepare.sh   - Linux/macOS 构建脚本（需网络）
- deploy/deploy.bat   - 服务器一键部署脚本（无需网络/pnpm）
- deploy/stop.bat     - 服务停止脚本
- deploy/README.txt   - 完整部署指南

部署脚本功能:
- Node.js 版本检查（>= v20）
- 部署文件完整性验证
- .env 配置自动创建
- 数据库目录创建与迁移（直接调用 node_modules 内的 prisma）
- 端口冲突检测与进程终止
- 健康检查等待（curl 优先，回退 PowerShell）
- 防火墙配置指南

后端修改:
- app.ts 增加前端静态文件托管（检测 frontend/dist 存在时启用）
- 支持 STATIC_DIR 环境变量自定义静态文件路径
- 非 /api 路由 fallback 到 index.html（支持 Vue Router history 模式）

Co-authored-by: Zzgj <Zzgj@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prepare 脚本构建顺序错误 - prisma generate 必须在 tsc build 之前

根因: TypeScript 编译需要 @prisma/client 导出的类型定义(PrismaClient,
AssetStatus, DeviceType 等), 这些类型由 prisma generate 生成到
node_modules/.prisma/client/. 原脚本先执行 tsc build(步骤4)再执行
prisma generate(步骤5), 导致 75 个 TS 编译错误.

修复: prepare.bat 和 prepare.sh 中将 prisma generate 移到 tsc build 之前.

Co-authored-by: Zzgj <Zzgj@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prepare 脚本 prisma generate 失败 - 缺少 .env 文件

根因: prisma.config.ts 通过 env('DATABASE_URL') 读取环境变量,
prisma generate 时会加载该配置. 构建机器上如果没有 backend/.env
文件, DATABASE_URL 未定义导致 PrismaConfigEnvError.

修复: 在 prisma generate 之前检查 .env 是否存在, 不存在则自动
从 .env.example 复制. prepare.bat 和 prepare.sh 同步修复.

Co-authored-by: Zzgj <Zzgj@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prepare.bat 注释乱码报错 + 组装阶段 xcopy 卡死

问题1: bat 文件中 :: 注释包含中文, 在部分 Windows 编码环境下
会被当作盘符标签解析, 导致第一行报错.
修复: 所有输出文本改为英文, 彻底避免编码问题.

问题2: xcopy 复制 node_modules(数万个小文件)极慢, 看起来像卡死.
修复: 改用 robocopy 并添加进度提示, 每个大步骤单独输出状态.

Co-authored-by: Zzgj <Zzgj@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prepare.bat 输出恢复中文, 移除所有 :: 注释避免乱码

根因: bat 中 :: 是标签语法而非真正的注释, 后接中文字符时
cmd 会尝试将其解析为命令导致报错. echo 输出的中文配合
chcp 65001 不受影响.

修复: 删除所有 :: 注释行, echo 输出全部恢复为中文.

Co-authored-by: Zzgj <Zzgj@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add GA4 tracking to consultations booking page (#1102)

* Add GA4 tracking to consultations booking: booking_modal_open, begin_checkout, booking_thank_you_view

Wire up the three missing analytics events on the consultations page to match
the existing event/MBA booking patterns:

- booking_modal_open: fires when user clicks the booking CTA, includes
  section_id, level label, and cohort date
- begin_checkout (ecommerce): pushes GA4 enhanced ecommerce event with
  consultation tier price and selection context
- InitiateCheckout (Meta Pixel): fires alongside booking_modal_open
- booking_thank_you_view: fires via useEffect when the thank-you modal
  opens after successful reservation

The free guides and resources page already has full GA4 coverage via
MediaForm (media_form_*) and SproutsSquadCommunity (community_signup_*)
components — no changes needed there.

Adds two focused test cases covering both essentials and deep-dive tier
analytics payloads.

525520074:

Update ga4-manual-setup-steps.md: add section 4 documenting all custom
dimensions/metrics, the automated registration script, and how to use
section_id to distinguish consultations vs MBA vs events booking flows
in GA4 reports.
